name: F & a
description_short: F&A Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving LA County public safety employees with competitive rates on loans, deposits, and credit products.
description_long:
F&A Credit Union is a federally-insured, member-owned credit union serving Los Angeles County residents employed in public safety and related fields. The organization operates under the tagline "Great rates. No gimmicks" and maintains a routing number (322078383) indicating NCUA insurance coverage. Founded to serve a specific membership community, F&A positions itself as an alternative to traditional banks with a focus on personalized service for those who "keep LA County safe and running."

F&A offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (up to $150,000 at rates as low as 4.25% APR), mortgages (30-year fixed at 6.125% APR), certificates of deposit (terms from 6 months to 5 years, APY up to 4.10%), checking and savings accounts with no monthly fees, credit cards (Premier Low Rate Visa at 6.90%-14.50% APR), personal loans, HELOCs, and recreational loans. They also provide protective products like Guaranteed Auto Protection and Mechanical Breakdown Protection.

F&A differentiates itself through member-exclusive benefits including high-yield checking accounts (APY up to 4.00%), no-fee accounts and services, flexible certificate terms with automatic renewal options, and community programs like members-only Shred Day events. The institution emphasizes relationship-based service and competitive rates tailored to its membership rather than pursuing a mass-market approach.

As a credit union, F&A's primary limitation is membership eligibility—access is restricted to those meeting their employment-based requirements. While rates are competitive, they are not universally the lowest in market (e.g., 6.125% mortgage rates are moderate rather than exceptional). The website does not provide comprehensive rate comparison data or detailed loan approval requirements, which may limit transparency for prospective members.
services: Auto loans (up to $150,000, rates from 4.25% APR, 84-month terms)
Certificates of deposit (6 months to 5 years, APY up to 4.10%)
High-Yield eChecking accounts (APY up to 4.00%, no monthly fees)
Premier Low Rate Visa credit cards (6.90%-14.50% APR, $0 annual fee)
Guaranteed Auto Protection and Mechanical Breakdown Protection
pros: No monthly fees on checking accounts and certificates, reducing banking costs
High-yield checking (APY up to 4.00%) turns primary account into earning tool when paperless eStatements elected
Auto loans available up to $150,000 with 84-month terms and rates as low as 4.25% APR
Guaranteed Auto Protection and Mechanical Breakdown Protection options provide comprehensive vehicle coverage
Certificates available with flexible 6-month to 5-year terms and no fees, with automatic renewal
Premier Low Rate Visa credit card offers flat 0.00% annual fee with competitive APR of 6.90%-14.50%
Free overdraft protection available when linked to F&A savings account
cons: Membership is restricted to LA County public safety employees and related eligible individuals, limiting accessibility
Mortgage rates (6.125% APR) are moderate and not exceptionally competitive compared to specialized lenders
Credit card credit line maximum of $25,000 may be limiting for higher-credit applicants
Website does not provide detailed membership eligibility criteria or membership application process
Limited transparency on auto loan approval requirements or vehicle age restrictions beyond 12-year maximum
best_for: Los Angeles County public safety employees seeking relationship-based banking with no-fee accounts
Members looking to finance vehicles with longer 84-month terms and protective coverage options
Savers interested in fixed-rate certificates with automatic renewal and no monthly maintenance fees
Credit union members seeking low-APR credit cards without annual fees
diagnosis: F&A Credit Union is best for Los Angeles County public safety employees and eligible family members seeking no-fee banking, competitive auto financing, and relationship-based service. The primary caveat is restricted membership eligibility—the institution is not open to the general public and requires membership qualifications based on employment or affiliation with LA County public safety agencies.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
